description.value:
Shorthand property for setting the custom `coh-font-fit` properties.

Note that setting only the min and max sizes will result in `coh-font-fit-mode` being set to `fit`.

Only supported in CoHTML (Ore UI).

Allows fitting text within a container by automatically changing the size.

Only supported in CoHTML (Ore UI).

values.name: none
values.description: Default - no changes to the font size will be attempted

values.name: fit
values.description: The text font size will be changed to fit its parent - grow or shrink

values.name: shrink
values.description: The text will shrink only its size if it can’t fit in the container

description.value:
Determines the minimum font size used to fit a text within a container.

Has a minimum effective value of 6px. Any less than that will be treated by the font fitting algorithm as if `coh-font-fit-min-size` was set to 6px.

Only supported in CoHTML (Ore UI).

Determines the maximum font size used to fit a text if `coh-font-fit-mode` is set to `fit`.

Has no effect if `coh-font-fit-mode` is not set to `fit`.

Only supported in CoHTML (Ore UI).

@font-face descriptor. Controls rasterization.

Defaults to `on`.

Please keep in mind that stroke is only supported with SDF rendering, hence you cannot turn off SDF for stroked text.

Only supported in CoHTML (Ore UI).

Controls how an element is rendered.

The `snap` tells CoHTML whether the element should be snapped to an integer coordinates. The `aa` tells the rendering library whether to generate anti-aliasing border for the element.

This property is for internal use only.

Only supported in CoHTML (Ore UI).

Element will not be snapped to integer coordinates.

Element will not have an anti-aliasing border generated for it.

Element will not be snapped to integer coordinates.

Element will automatically determine whether to have an anti-aliasing border generated for it.

Element will not be snapped to integer coordinates.

Element will have an anti-aliasing border generated for it.

Element will automatically determine whether to be snapped to integer coordinates.

Element will not have an anti-aliasing border generated for it.

Element will automatically determine whether to be snapped to integer coordinates.

Element will automatically determine whether to have an anti-aliasing border generated for it.

Element will automatically determine whether to be snapped to integer coordinates.

Element will have an anti-aliasing border generated for it.

Element will be snapped to integer coordinates.

Element will not have an anti-aliasing border generated for it.

Element will be snapped to integer coordinates.

Element will automatically determine whether to have an anti-aliasing border generated for it.

Element will be snapped to integer coordinates.

Element will have an anti-aliasing border generated for it.

The element is drawn on the screen through the client’s compositor.

For more details see the [documentation on the Compositor](https://docs.coherent-labs.com/cpp-gameface/integration/optional_features/compositor/)

Only supported in CoHTML (Ore UI).

The element is rendered through the UI surface partitioning flow. For more details see the [documentation on the UI Surface Partitioning](https://docs.coherent-labs.com/cpp-gameface/integration/optional_features/uisurfacepartitioning/)

Only supported in CoHTML (Ore UI).

values.name: on
values.description: The element is rendered through the UI surface partitioning flow.

values.name: off
values.description: The element is not rendered through the UI surface partitioning flow.
